slides.title: Repeat Until You Say Stop
slides.body: A `while` loop runs a block of code **over and over** as long as a condition stays `True`. Think of pouring water into a glass — you keep pouring *while* it isn't full.
slides.tip: while loops are perfect when you don't know in advance how many times you'll need to repeat.

slides.title: Your First while Loop
slides.body: Set a **counter**, check it in the condition, and **update it** inside the loop. Python re-checks the condition after every pass.
slides.code:
count = 1
while count <= 3:
    print("Hello!", count)
    count += 1
slides.tip: count += 1 is shorthand for count = count + 1.

slides.title: The Danger: Infinite Loops
slides.body: If the condition **never becomes `False`**, the loop runs forever and freezes your program. Always make sure something inside the loop moves you toward stopping.
slides.code:
count = 1
while count <= 5:
    print(count)
    # Oops — forgot count += 1!
slides.tip: Stuck in an infinite loop? Press Ctrl + C to escape.

slides.title: Exit Early with break
slides.body: `break` lets you jump out of a loop immediately — no matter what the condition says. It's the emergency exit inside your loop.
slides.tip: while True: combined with break is a clean pattern for input validation.

slides.title: You've Got It!
slides.body: `while` loops repeat **until a condition turns `False`**. Remember the three keys: **initialize**, **check**, **update** — and use `break` to exit early when needed.
slides.tip: Use for when you know the count; use while when you repeat until something changes.
